title: 223 By BP
description: This dataset comprises laser-evoked pain responses from participants who received fixed-intensity nociceptive stimuli at discrete energy levels (low pain: 3J and 3.5J; high pain: 3.5J and 4J) while providing subjective pain intensity ratings. The study involved 30 laser stimulation trials organized across 3 blocks with concurrent neurophysiological recordings, designed to investigate the neural correlates of pain perception and subjective pain rating behavior.
methods_description: Participants received laser stimulation at fixed discrete intensities (low pain: 3J and 3.5J; high pain: 3.5J and 4J) organized in 3 blocks of 10 stimuli each, with high and low pain stimuli evenly distributed. Following each stimulus, participants provided pain intensity ratings on a 0-10 scale (0=no sensation, 4=pain onset, 6=moderate pain, 8=severe pain, 10=unbearable pain). Participants were instructed to focus on the laser stimulation, keep their eyes open, and fix their gaze on the crosshairs displayed on the screen. A 5-second inter-stimulus interval separated each trial, with subsequent trials beginning at random intervals after pain rating completion.
